name: Formaldehyde
note: Known human carcinogen; the EU has banned or severely restricted it across consumer products.

name: Naphthalene
note: Traditional mothball ingredient banned in EU consumer products since 2008 (possible carcinogen); still sold in the US.

name: Sodium tripolyphosphate
note: Water-softening phosphate banned in EU consumer laundry (2013) and dishwasher (2017) detergents over waterway damage; still common in US products.

name: Methyldibromo glutaronitrile
note: Preservative banned in EU cosmetics and heavily restricted elsewhere after an epidemic of contact allergy.

name: Triclosan
note: Antibacterial with endocrine and resistance concerns; the EU refused its approval for several biocidal uses and the US FDA banned it from antiseptic washes.

name: Methylisothiazolinone
note: Preservative and major contact allergen; EU requires allergen labeling on detergents at low thresholds.

name: Methylchloroisothiazolinone
note: Preservative allergen; EU-restricted with mandatory labeling.

name: Benzisothiazolinone
note: Isothiazolinone preservative; a growing cause of contact allergy, with EU labeling requirements.

name: Glutaraldehyde
note: Disinfectant and potent respiratory/skin sensitizer; tightly restricted in the EU.

name: Boric acid
note: Classified in the EU as toxic to reproduction; consumer uses are restricted. Appears in US pest-control and cleaning products.

name: Borax
note: Sodium borate, same EU reproductive-toxicity classification as boric acid; a common US 'natural' cleaning ingredient.

name: 1,4-Dichlorobenzene
note: Mothball and deodorizer-block chemical; possible carcinogen, banned in EU air fresheners and toilet blocks.

name: Phthalate
note: Plasticizer family used in fragrance carriers and plastics; several members are EU-banned reproductive toxicants.

name: NTA (nitrilotriacetic acid)
note: Phosphate-substitute chelator; possible carcinogen classification in the EU.

name: 2-Butoxyethanol
note: Glycol-ether solvent in degreasers and glass cleaners; EU limits workplace and consumer exposure.

name: Sodium hypochlorite
note: Effective disinfectant but corrosive to skin and lungs — and it forms toxic chloramine gas if mixed with ammonia or acids. Never combine cleaning products.

name: Ammonia
note: Strong alkaline cleaner; respiratory irritant, and mixing with bleach creates toxic chloramine gas.

name: Hydrochloric acid
note: Corrosive acid in toilet-bowl and masonry cleaners; serious burn and fume hazard, never mix with bleach.

name: Sulfuric acid
note: Highly corrosive drain-opener acid; among the most dangerous consumer chemicals to skin and eyes.

name: Sodium hydroxide
note: Caustic base in oven and drain cleaners; corrosive on contact, safe once rinsed away.

name: Potassium hydroxide
note: Caustic base similar to lye; corrosive on contact.

name: Sodium metasilicate
note: Strongly alkaline builder in dishwasher detergents; corrosive in concentrated form.

name: Benzalkonium chloride
note: Quaternary ammonium disinfectant ('quat'); skin/lung irritant with growing resistance concerns.

name: Didecyldimethylammonium chloride
note: Quaternary ammonium disinfectant; respiratory and skin irritant.

name: Chloroxylenol
note: Antiseptic in some disinfectants; toxic to cats and a mild sensitizer.

name: Pine oil
note: Traditional disinfectant fragrance; can irritate skin and is toxic to pets in concentrated form.

name: Fragrance
note: Undisclosed scent mixture; the EU detergent regulation forces 26 allergens to be named on the label, the US does not.

name: Limonene
note: Citrus solvent and fragrance; oxidizes into skin sensitizers, EU-labeled allergen.

name: Linalool
note: Fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen when oxidized.

name: Hexyl cinnamal
note: Fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Citronellol
note: Fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Geraniol
note: Fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Benzyl salicylate
note: Fragrance component; EU-labeled allergen.

name: Optical brighteners
note: Fluorescent dyes that stay on fabric to make whites look brighter; slow to biodegrade, occasional skin irritation.

name: Monoethanolamine
note: Alkaline solvent in heavy-duty cleaners; respiratory and skin irritant.

name: Triethanolamine
note: pH adjuster with nitrosamine-formation potential.

name: EDTA
note: Chelating water softener; poorly biodegradable, low human-health concern.

name: Phosphonates
note: Phosphate-replacement water softeners; environmental persistence is the main concern.

name: Ethoxylated surfactant
note: Ethoxylated ingredients can carry manufacturing traces of 1,4-dioxane (a probable carcinogen); reputable makers strip it out. New York State caps 1,4-dioxane in cleaning products.

name: Sodium laureth sulfate
note: Common cleaning surfactant; mild irritant, 1,4-dioxane traces depend on manufacturing quality.

name: Sodium lauryl sulfate
note: Foaming surfactant; skin irritant at high concentrations.

name: Linear alkylbenzene sulfonate
note: The workhorse laundry surfactant; biodegradable, mild irritant.

name: Enzymes (protease/amylase/lipase)
note: Effective stain removers; powdered forms can sensitize airways, liquids are low concern.

name: Isopropyl alcohol
note: Fast-evaporating solvent; flammable, drying to skin.

name: Acetone
note: Solvent; flammable and drying but low systemic toxicity.

name: Butane/propane propellant
note: Aerosol propellants; flammable VOCs, an inhalation hazard in enclosed spaces.

name: Hydrogen peroxide
note: Oxygen-based bleach; irritating when concentrated, breaks down into water and oxygen.

name: Sodium percarbonate
note: Powdered oxygen bleach that releases hydrogen peroxide; the safer modern alternative to chlorine bleach.

name: Sodium carbonate
note: Alkaline cleaning builder; irritant as a raw powder, benign in use.

name: Sodium bicarbonate
note: Gentle abrasive and deodorizer. No concern.

name: Citric acid
note: Descaling acid. No concern.

name: Acetic acid
note: Vinegar-based cleaner. No concern beyond the smell.

name: Lactic acid
note: Mild descaling and antibacterial acid. Low concern.

name: Soap (fatty acid salts)
note: Traditional saponified cleaner. No concern.

name: Alkyl polyglucosides
note: Sugar-derived surfactants; among the mildest and most biodegradable available.

name: Cocamidopropyl betaine
note: Mild surfactant; allergies trace to impurities.

name: Zeolites
note: Mineral water softeners that replaced phosphates in EU detergents. No concern.

name: PEG compound
note: Polyethylene glycol family; 1,4-dioxane manufacturing traces are the watch item.

name: Paraben
note: Paraben preservative; the EU bans five members of the family in cosmetics.

name: Benzophenone
note: UV stabilizer; possible carcinogen (IARC 2B).

name: Polyquaternium
note: Film-forming polymer; low health concern.

name: PPG compound
note: Polypropylene glycol family; similar profile to PEGs.

name: Silicone (siloxane)
note: Silicone family; inert in use, environmental persistence varies.

name: Acrylate polymer
note: Synthetic polymer; counted as a microplastic in some EU assessments.

name: Colorant
note: Dye added purely for appearance; unnecessary in a cleaning product.
